summary: Decompiled the first three functions from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s into a new ftCommon source unit, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C35.c, reaching a reported 94.58% file match. The PR added ftCo_800C3598, ftCo_800C36DC, and ftCo_800C37A0 as C/ASM hybrid functions, wired the new object into obj_files.mk, moved their declarations from the placeholder ft_0C31.h into a dedicated ftCo_0C35.h, expanded ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x648-0x654, exposed ftCo_Damage_CalcVel for cross-file use, and added small missing prototypes in item/mp collision headers. It also improved tools/report_score.py with argparse and a --title mode used to generate PR titles like this one.
what_changed: Removed the ftCo_800C3598, ftCo_800C36DC, and ftCo_800C37A0 assembly blocks and their associated literals/assert strings from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C35.c with C implementations guarded by ASM/MUST_MATCH fallback assembly for the three migrated functions.
Added ftCo_0C35.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES before the remaining ft_0C31.s.o so the migrated text is built from C while the rest of ft_0C31.s remains assembly.
Created ftCo_0C35.h declaring ftCo_800C3598, ftCo_800C36DC, and ftCo_800C37A0, then removed those declarations from the broader placeholder ft_0C31.h.
Included ftCo_0C35.h from ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_DamageSong.c, ftCo_Guard.c, and fighter.c to satisfy callers after moving the declarations.
Changed ftCo_Damage_CalcVel from a static-style declaration/implementation to an exported ASM-compatible function so ftCo_0C35.c can call it.
Expanded struct ftCommonData at offsets 0x648 through 0x654 from an opaque byte span into named fields x648, x64C, x650, and x654 used by the new C code.
Added placeholder prototypes for it_802A7AF0, it_802A7AAC, it_802A7B34, mpColl_800443C4, and mpColl_80044164 in headers touched by the new include dependency set.
Updated tools/report_score.py to use argparse, accept an object path via args.obj_path, and add a --title mode that prints a concise PR-title-style match summary.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated setup shared by ftCo_800C3598 and ftCo_800C36DC into static inline inlineA0, matching the recurring sequence that initializes dmg.x18F4 from p_ftCommonData->x648, clamps dmg.x1954, sets x2220_flag.bits.b4, and calls Fighter_UnkRecursiveFunc_8006D044.

Kept MUST_MATCH fallback assembly inside each migrated function rather than deleting all reference code, allowing progress with a high non-perfect score while preserving exact assembly for match builds.
Promoted ftCo_Damage_CalcVel to a reusable non-static symbol instead of duplicating its velocity merge logic in the new ftCo_0C35 unit.
Used named struct fields for p_ftCommonData offsets instead of continuing to access an opaque byte array, making the timer and tuning constants at x648/x64C/x650/x654 reusable for future decomp work.

Used existing higher-level helpers such as ftParts_8007500C, ftParts_80075AF0, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, GET_FIGHTER, and ftCo_Damage_CalcAngle/Cos/Sin flow in the C version of the animation/velocity logic.
Moved only the completed address range into the new source file and left the remaining ft_0C31.s functions in assembly, minimizing risk and preserving build order.
decomp_lessons: When splitting functions out of a large assembly file, create a narrow header for the migrated range and update include sites rather than leaving newly decompiled functions declared in a catch-all placeholder header.
Small ftCommonData expansions can unlock readable decompilation: replacing an opaque byte span with typed fields at exact offsets made the C logic around x648, x64C, x650, and x654 expressible.

For partially matching functions, an ASM function with a C body under !MUST_MATCH/WIP and exact inline assembly under MUST_MATCH can preserve build correctness while documenting the intended C structure.
Common repeated assembly prefixes often indicate an inline helper; here the shared initialization in ftCo_800C3598 and ftCo_800C36DC was captured as inlineA0.
Literal placement matters for matching: the new C file explicitly defines S32_TO_F32, zero, M_PI, M_PI_2, and assert strings formerly emitted from the assembly file.
Decompiling one file may require making earlier C functions externally visible; ftCo_Damage_CalcVel had to lose its static treatment so later ftCommon code could call it.
naming_conventions: New decompiled symbols retained address-based names: ftCo_800C3598, ftCo_800C36DC, ftCo_800C37A0.
The dedicated header uses an address-derived include guard, GALE01_0C3598.
Unknown ftCommonData fields were named by offset, such as x648, x64C, x650, and x654, matching common project practice until semantics are identified.
Temporary literals retained linker-style names such as ftCo_804D8CD0, ftCo_804D8CD8, ftCo_804D8CE0, ftCo_804D8CE8, ftCo_804D3C38, ftCo_804D3C40, and lbl_803C6CA8.
The local helper name inlineA0 is non-semantic and evidence suggests it was used as a matching tactic rather than a finalized domain name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used ASM function declarations with C bodies for ordinary builds and inline assembly fallbacks for MUST_MATCH/non-WIP builds.
Inserted self-assignments such as fp->dmg.x18F4 = fp->dmg.x18F4 and fp->xF0_ground_kb_vel = fp->xF0_ground_kb_vel to influence generated code/register behavior.
Preserved explicit literals and assert strings in the C file to replace matching data/sdata2 entries removed from ft_0C31.s.
Modeled signed integer to float conversion through a S32_TO_F32 double constant, mirroring the original xoris/lfd conversion pattern.
Used M_PI and M_PI_2 constants for rotation calculations corresponding to old sdata2 literals ftCo_804D8CE0 and ftCo_804D8CE8.
Kept #pragma peephole on after inline assembly blocks, indicating CodeWarrior-specific matching control.
Used HSD_JObjSetRotationY in C to express the original direct rotation-y store plus dirty-matrix/assert behavior around JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match ftCo_800C37A0 or rename inlineA0 to a semantic helper.
Search later changes to ftCommonData x648/x64C/x650/x654 to see if these fields were given real names.
Find PRs that continue migrating functions from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s after ftCo_0C35.c.o was inserted.
Search for later cleanup of ftCo_Damage_CalcVel's ASM wrapper or further cross-file uses.
Check whether the placeholder prototypes added in itlinkhookshot.h and mpcoll.h were later typed.
